data.title: Brambles Price Target Raised 1.8% to A$17.75/Share by Jefferies
Insulet beats quarterly profit estimates on insulin pump demand
data.description:
Insulet reported a higher-than-expected profit in the fourth quarter on Thursday, driven by strong demand for its wearable insulin pumps.

The company produces and sells Omnipod, an automated insulin delivery device, which works without the need for external tubing typically associated with conventional pumps and eliminates the need for multiple daily injections for people with insulin-dependent diabetes.

Insulet's Omnipod 5 device is the first such system to be cleared for use in managing both type 1 and type 2 diabetes.

The medical device maker has been working to ease investor concerns about the potential hit on its insulin pump sales, given the soaring demand for the newer diabetes and weight-loss drugs categorized as GLP-1s.

In September, Insulet said it expects new customer additions to be stronger in the second half of 2024 compared to the same period last year.

The company expects its annual 2025 revenue to grow between 16% and 20%.

The Acton, Massachusetts-based company's total revenue jumped 17.2% to $597.5 million for the quarter ended December 31, beating analysts' estimates of $582.8 million.

Sales of Omnipod insulin pumps came in at $585.7 million for the fourth quarter, compared with analysts' estimates of $576 million. It expects Omnipod revenue growth in the range of 17% to 21% for 2025.

On an adjusted basis, the company earned $1.15 per share, above analysts' average estimate of $1.02.

Insulet's larger peer Dexcom beat fourth-quarter sales earlier this month and reiterated its annual sales forecast.

data.title: Globant Q4 Adjusted Earnings, Revenue Rise; Sets Outlook -- Shares Fall
data.description:
Globant reported Q4 adjusted earnings late Thursday of $1.75 per diluted share, up from $1.62 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected $1.74.

Revenue for the quarter that ended Dec. 31 was $642.5 million, up from $580.7 million a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $644.3 million.

The company said it expects adjusted EPS of $1.55 to $1.63 for Q1 and $6.80 to $7.20 for the full year. Analysts expected $1.66 for the quarter and $7.34 for the year.

Globant expects revenue of $618 million to $628 million for Q1 and $2.64 billion to $2.71 billion for the full-year. Analysts expected $638 million for Q1 and $2.74 billion for 2024.

Globant shares were down about 10% in after-hours activity.

data.title: Earnings Flash (BBDC) Barings BDC Q4 Net Investment Income $0.28 vs. FactSet Est of $0.29
Yancoal Australia Posts Lower 2024 Earnings, Revenue
data.description:
Yancoal Australia reported Thursday that its 2024 earnings per diluted share fell to AU$0.921 from AU$1.371 a year earlier.

Revenue for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was AU$6.86 billion, down from AU$7.78 billion a year earlier. Analysts surveyed by Visible Alpha expected AU$6.86 billion.

The company expects fiscal 2025 attributable saleable coal production of 35 million to 39 million tonnes, with attributable capital expenditure of AU$750 million to AU$900 million.

The board declared an interim dividend of AU$0.52 per share, up from AU$0.325 a year earlier, payable on April 30 to shareholders on record as of March 14.

data.title: HEADS UP: FED GOV KUGLER REMARKS COMING UP AT 5P ET
Con Edison Reports 2024 Earnings
data.description:
Consolidated Edison, Inc. (Con Edison), one of the nation's largest investor-owned energy-delivery companies, has released its financial results for the year 2024. The company, which provides a wide range of energy-related products and services through its subsidiaries, has detailed its performance in its latest SEC 8-K filing.

Financial Highlights

Con Edison reported a net income for common stock of $1,820 million or $5.26 per share for 2024, compared to $2,519 million or $7.25 per share in 2023. Adjusted earnings (non-GAAP) were $1,868 million or $5.40 per share in 2024, up from $1,762 million or $5.07 per share in 2023. For the fourth quarter of 2024, net income for common stock was $310 million or $0.90 per share, compared to $335 million or $0.97 per share in the same period in 2023. Adjusted earnings for the fourth quarter were $340 million or $0.98 per share, compared to $346 million or $1.00 per share in the 2023 period.

Business and Operational Highlights

Con Edison highlighted several operational achievements in 2024, including the effective implementation of its strategy to support customers' adoption of clean energy technologies while maintaining reliable electric service. The company broke ground on key substations and advanced new transmission lines under its Reliable Clean City program. Additionally, Con Edison saw an increase in demand for electrification, driven by new construction and policy requirements for clean heat in new buildings.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

Con Edison plans significant capital investments in the coming years, with $5,122 million expected in 2025 and $8,067 million in 2026. From 2027 to 2029, the company anticipates capital investments totaling $24,469 million. These investments will be funded through internally-generated funds, long-term debt, and common equity issuance. The company also completed an equity forward transaction in the fourth quarter of 2024, providing greater pricing certainty for a portion of its 2025 equity funding needs.

Management's Perspective

Tim Cawley, Chairman and CEO of Con Edison, expressed optimism about the company's growth and its ability to meet the increasing demand for electrification. He emphasized the company's commitment to supporting the clean energy transition and maintaining reliable service. Kirk Andrews, Senior Vice President and CFO, highlighted the strong execution within the utility rate plans and the successful equity forward transaction as key drivers of the company's financial performance in 2024.

Future Outlook

For 2025, Con Edison expects adjusted earnings per share to be in the range of $5.50 to $5.70. The company also forecasts a five-year compounded annual adjusted earnings per share growth rate of 6% to 7% based on its 2025 guidance. Con Edison plans to issue up to $1,750 million of long-term debt in 2025 and up to $3,800 million in 2026, with additional common equity issuance planned for the coming years.

SEC Filing: CONSOLIDATED EDISON INC [ ED ] - 8-K - Feb. 20, 2025

data.title: CoStar Targets Australia's Domain With $1.68B Takeover Proposal
data.description:
(21:40 GMT) *Australia's Domain Receives Takeover Proposal From CoStar
(21:40 GMT) *Domain: Nasdaq-Listed CoStar Proposing A$4.20/Share in Cash
(21:41 GMT) *Domain: CoStar Has Acquired a 16.9% Stake at A$4.20/Share
(21:41 GMT) *Domain Is Assessing CoStar's Indicative Proposal
(21:43 GMT) *Domain: CoStar's Proposal Conditions Include Due Diligence
(21:55 GMT) CoStar Targets Australia's Domain With $1.68B Takeover Proposal

By Stuart Condie

SYDNEY--U.S. real-estate advertiser and analytics provider CoStar has proposed a $1.68 billion takeover of Domain, Australia's second-largest property advertiser.

Domain on Friday said that CoStar, which operates U.S. portals including Homes.com, made an indicative cash proposal worth 4.20 Australian dollars, equivalent to about $2.66. That represents a near 35% premium to Domain's share price before the local share-market open.

Domain said its board would assess the proposal. CoStar has already acquired a near 17% stake at the proposed offer price, Domain said.

Write to Stuart Condie at stuart.condie@wsj.com

data.title: Celsius Shares Soared on Earnings Beat and Alani Nu Acquisition — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Evie Liu

Celsius Holdings stock shot up nearly 15% on Wednesday after the energy drink maker abruptly said it would report earnings on Thursday and participate in a well-known consumer analyst conference on Friday, where executives will make a presentation.

Such announcements of earnings report dates and conference participation don't usually move stocks. But companies alert investors of such plans in advance. Celsius' sudden notice seems to have led the market to believe that it has good news — something much needed for the stock that has been battered over the past few months. Celsius couldn't immediately be reached for comment.

Celsius shares have slumped since May as the fast-growing brand ran into trouble with its expansion plans. PepsiCo, its major distributor in the U.S., reduced orders as it adjusts the amount of inventory it holds, hurting Celsius' financial results.

For the three months ended in September, Celsius reported earnings and revenue that both missed Wall Street estimates. Total sales declined 30% from the year-ago quarter and the company barely broke even, compared with per-share earnings of 30 cents a year ago.

Investors are concerned that weakness in consumer spending could bring headwinds to the energy drink sector as well. Dominating brands like Red Bull and Monster Beverage are fighting to win back lost market share, while new rivals are growing in popularity.

For the fourth quarter of 2024, analysts polled by FactSet expect Celsius to post $328 million in sales, down more than 5% from the year-ago period, and earnings to come at 11 cents per share compared with 17 cents a year ago.

As Pepsi's supply-chain adjustment settles down, Wall Street expects Celsius to start recovering in 2025. Rather than the quarterly results, investors will closely watch what management says about its 2025 outlook on Thursday's earnings call, and at Friday's presentation at the Consumer Analyst Group of New York Conference.

Write to Evie Liu at evie.liu@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: RevSparkAI and SEQUESTO Redefine RFP Response Automation with AI-Driven Solutions in the US
data.description:
Revolutionizing Business Processes with Cutting-Edge AI Technology

Los Angeles, California--(Newsfile Corp. - February 20, 2025) - RevSparkAI, a leader in leveraging data and advanced technology for transformative business outcomes, has partnered with SEQUESTO, the globally leading AI-first platform for RFP Response Automation, to bring SEQUESTO's groundbreaking technology to the United States. This strategic collaboration between two innovation-driven companies marks a major step forward in revolutionizing how businesses handle the RFP process and business processes in general.

SEQUESTO's AI-first platform is engineered to streamline and elevate every aspect of the RFP response workflow. By integrating advanced AI with a human-centered approach, the SEQUESTO platform transforms the traditionally complex and labor-intensive RFP response process into an efficient, effective, AI-powered, and results-driven experience. This innovative solution enables businesses to allocate resources strategically, reduce operational costs, and focus on growth.

This strategic partnership emphasizes a shared commitment to advancing technology and delivering unparalleled value to clients. SEQUESTO's expertise in RFP response automation and innovation perfectly complements RevSparkAI's mission to enhance business operations through intelligent, AI-driven solutions. Together, they are setting a new standard for efficiency and innovation in RFP response management.

"The launch of our partnership with SEQUESTO's AI RFP Response Automation platform in the US is a testament to RevSparkAI's dedication to innovation and excellence. Partnering with SEQUESTO allows us to deliver a transformative solution to businesses, helping them optimize their RFP processes and achieve strategic goals more effectively and fostering growth." Brennon Cardone, Managing Partner of RevSparkAI

"Our strategic partnership with RevSparkAI unites two exceptional teams with deep expertise in AI-driven solutions and RFX Response Automation. Together, we are committed to delivering transformative solutions that empower organizations to streamline processes, enhance competitiveness, and unlock new efficiencies in an increasingly demanding market. With the combined strength of SEQUESTO's industry-leading platform and RevSparkAI's technological excellence, we are uniquely positioned to help businesses achieve their full potential and drive success." Patrick Dalvinck, CEO & Co-Founder, SEQUESTO

The SEQUESTO platform is designed to meet the needs of businesses across diverse industries, offering customizable features that cater to specific requirements. Its AI algorithms enable fast and accurate data analysis, driving improvements in proposal quality and efficiency. The platform's capabilities not only enhance productivity but also provide businesses with a significant competitive edge in their markets.

As RevSparkAI continues its expansion in the United States, the partnership with SEQUESTO solidifies a joint mission to push the boundaries of technological innovation. Together, they are empowering businesses with the solutions to thrive in an increasingly competitive and dynamic landscape.

For more information about RevSparkAI, please visit https://www.revsparkai.com

To learn more about SEQUESTO, please visit https://www.sequesto.com

For media inquiries, please contact pr@sequesto.com

About RevSparkAI

RevSparkAI pioneers AI-driven innovation and sustainable design, helping businesses optimize operations and drive revenue growth. Founded by industry experts with a history of delivering transformative solutions for global enterprises, the company specializes in AI integration, project management, vendor strategy, and executive sales. Committed to integrity and innovation, RevSparkAI empowers organizations to navigate complex challenges and achieve strategic success.

For more information, visit https://www.revsparkai.com.

Press Inquiries

Brennon Cardone info@revsparkai.com

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/241661

data.title: US airlines challenge Biden wheelchair passenger protection rule
data.description:
Major U.S. airlines and a trade association have challenged a Biden administration rule issued in December requiring new consumer protections for disabled passengers using wheelchairs.

United Airlines , Delta Air Lines , American Airlines , Southwest Airlines , JetBlue Airways and airline trade group Airlines for America challenge the rule in the 5th Circuit U.S. Court of Appeals that set stricter standards for accommodating passengers with wheelchairs and requires carriers to reimburse passengers for damage to wheelchairs.

Then-Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg said last year the rule was the biggest expansion of rights for wheelchair users since 2008 and makes it easier for USDOT to hold airlines accountable for damage to wheelchairs or any delay in their return.

Airlines for America said carriers have been making meaningful progress enhancing services for passengers with disabilities since signing a commitment in 2022.

The airline group said it was not challenging all aspects of the regulation but said "certain provisions of the final rule go beyond the USDOT’s statutory authority, violate the Administrative Procedure Act and represent regulatory overreach by the Biden Administration."

The USDOT rule says airlines must return checked wheelchairs in the same condition or face the presumption they are responsible, but carriers can challenge it.

Under the final rule, airlines must notify passengers whose wheelchairs have been mishandled of their rights, provide loaners and quickly repair or replace broken wheelchairs as well as reimburse passengers for transportation costs incurred as a result of a passenger’s wheelchair being delayed by the airline.

In October, USDOT fined American Airlines a record $50 million for its treatment of disabled passengers, including failing to provide some with adequate assistance and mishandling wheelchairs.

USDOT says an estimated 5.5 million Americans use a wheelchair and data shows that for every 100 wheelchairs or scooters transported on domestic flights at least one is damaged, delayed, or lost.

data.title: Qube Holdings Price Target Raised 1.9% to A$4.92/Share by Jefferies
Booking Holdings 4Q Earnings Top Expectations
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Booking Holdings blew past Wall Street's expectations as the company followed in the footsteps of its competitors with higher-than-expected earnings and bookings.

The travel company, which owns brands such as Booking.com and Kayak, posted a profit of $1.07 billion, or $31.95 a share, in the three-months ended Dec. 31, compared with $222 million, or $6.28 a share, a year earlier.

Stripping out certain one-time items, adjusted per-share earnings were $41.55, ahead of the $36.08 forecast by analysts, according to FactSet.

Revenue rose 14% to $5.47 billion. Analysts surveyed by FactSet forecast revenue of $5.18 billion.

The number of nights booked in hotel rooms grew 13% to 261 million, ahead of the 250 million expected by Wall Street, the Norwalk, Conn., company said. Gross bookings increased 17%.

Booking's competitors Expedia and Airbnb also posted better-than-expected financials that showed travel demand is still strong, even though some were expecting there to be a lull after a spike in business in 2021 and 2022.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Financial Services. Exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1618 ET - The march toward $3,000 continues for gold, with the front-month contract settling up 0.7% to a record $2,940 per troy ounce. Global economic uncertainty continues to provide upside support for gold. While the inflation picture has improved worldwide, it remains an issue, and President Trump's tariff threats add volatility, although analysts hope they are still only a leverage tool. "We await further gyrations in the Administration's tariff policies, but still believe that, at least in part, Trump will use tariffs as a negotiating tool to motivate countries to cooperate with his key priorities," Citi says in a note. SPDR Gold gains 0.1%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1445 ET - Walmart's warning of slower sales gains, together with slowdown comparable sales growth during the holiday quarter, is sparking concerns on Wall Street. The retail giant's 4.6% growth in U.S. comparable sales in the fourth quarter from a 5.3% increase in the third quarter shows lower prices haven't been enough to drive sentiment higher among Americans. "We're growing profit faster than sales," CEO Douglas McMillon says on a call with analysts, noting that Walmart has runway to scale our higher-margin businesses like membership, marketplace and advertising. Its wholesale business, Sam's Club, also saw a sequential deceleration in comparable sales in the period, driving Costco Wholesale shares 2.4% lower. Shares of Amazon, Target and Kroger are down between 1.5% and 2%, while Walmart drops 6.5%. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

1045 ET - U.S. home values rose $2.5 trillion in 2024, reaching $49.7 trillion, a 5.2% increase--the slowest since 2019--according to Redfin. More homes are for sale, creating buyer's markets in many areas, yet prices continue to climb monthly. Redfin predicts steady price growth in 2025 due to buyer competition over limited listings compared to pre-pandemic levels. Over the past decade, U.S. home values have more than doubled from $23 trillion in 2014, peaking at $50.4 trillion in July 2024. The slight drop reflects seasonal trends, with values rising March to September and dipping in winter. New construction is also boosting market valuation. (chris.wack@wsj.com)

0819 ET - Italian lender Banco BPM should get regulatory approval to use the so-called Danish compromise in its acquisition of asset manager Anima Holding, Deutsche Bank says in a research note. The European directive allows banks to reduce the hit to their capital when purchasing asset management businesses using their insurance subsidiaries. BPM raised its offer for Anima to 7 euros per share and said it is willing to go ahead in any case, prompting UniCredit, its larger peer who launched its own takeover offer, to threaten to pull its bid. "Approval of the Danish Compromise is a matter of "when" not "if", in our view--and we believe its benefits should be reflected in the terms of Unicredit's offer," analyst Giovanni Razzoli writes. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0650 ET - Commerzbank markets a dual-tranche issue of euro-denominated benchmark three- and 10-year covered bonds Thursday. The three-year bond is a public sector covered bond, or oeffentlicher Pfandbrief, maturing in February 2028. The spread is set at midswaps plus 22 basis points, versus initial price talk of around midswaps plus 28bps, a lead manager involved in the deal says. The 10-year bond is a mortgage covered bond, or Hypothekenpfandbrief, maturing in February 2035. The spread is set at midswaps plus 46bps, versus initial price talk in the area of midswaps plus 53bps. Demand so far for each bond is more than 2.3 billion euros, the lead manager says. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

0630 ET - Lloyds Banking Group's strategy has been primarily focused on organic growth since the start of its midterm plan, CEO Charlie Nunn said in a results call with reporters when asked about M&A opportunities. The British lender has done two small deals: It bought investment platform Embark in 2021 and car financing company Tusker in 2023. "We've always said capability and infill acquisitions where they bring something new that we can really bring to our broader customer base is part of our strategy, but anything more fundamental transformation really isn't part of the next two years. And that's because we're seeing just great growth," he added. This growth in deposits, loans, assets under management and in its pension business is larger than many other companies' acquisitions, he notes. Shares rise 5%. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0454 ET - A center-right government would be the most favorable toward a deal between Italy's UniCredit and German lender Commerzbank, Morningstar analyst Michael Field says in a market comment ahead of Sunday's elections in Germany. "Their voter base is generally less concerned about protecting workers from job losses at all costs as a result of a takeover," he notes. UniCredit has recently built up a 28% stake in Commerzbank and signaled its intention to pursue a tie-up, triggering political opposition in Berlin. The Alliance 90/The Greens have been the most open toward a deal but their inclusion in a coalition isn't guaranteed, Field says. The parties that are likely to enter the parliament have voiced concerns about the Italian lender's approach, he adds. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0444 ET - Share buybacks cannot be done every year, the CEO of Zurich Insurance says in a results call with reporters. The Swiss insurer paid out a 28 Swiss franc dividend per share for 2024, corresponding to its 75% profit payout policy. "There is no space with such a dividend policy to have a yearly buyback," Mario Greco said. The company will conduct buybacks in the case of extraordinary transactions, as seen two years ago, or if it accumulates cash and excess capital that it needs to return. This echoes the strategy Zurich outlined in November when it laid out 2025-2027 targets. In the same call, CFO Claudia Cordioli flagged "extremely attractive opportunities" to invest capital back in the business to support growth. Shares rise 3% to highs last seen in 2001. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0413 ET - NN Group's results show the reinsurer's capital position remains strong, KBW analyst Michele Ballatore says in a research note. NN group reported a solvency II ratio---a key measure of capital strength--of 194%, 9% ahead of what analysts had forecast, Ballatore says. While markets took their toll as expected, NN Group's management actions positively offset those effects, which is reflected in the 300 million-euro share buyback confirmed for next year, the analyst notes. The operating capital generation for the second half was 3% ahead of expectations, although consensus for the metric won't likely change after results, he says. "The operating capital generation disclosure by source shows a positive development at the investment return line and likely a higher than expected contribution from non-life," he says. Shares rise 6.6% to 48.61 euros. (maitane.sardon@wsj.com)

0308 ET - Lloyds Banking Group's update included comforting results though there were a lot of parts to digest, Jefferies says in a research note. The British bank took a provision linked to its motor finance business and issued guidance for 2025. Its 2026 guidance, which is more than 15% higher than consensus, was maintained. "The market may start to take more comfort in this today, given guidance that hedge income is expected to increase circa 2.7 billion pounds between 2024 and 2026," analyst Jonathan Pierce and associate Priya Rathod write. The motor finance risk will hang over the shares for a little longer, they add. Shares open 2% higher at around 64 pence. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0154 ET - EFG International's share drop after impressive results was surprising, Citi analysts say in a research note after the Swiss private bank's update on Wednesday. Shares opened higher and reverted course during the trading session to close down 2.7% at 13.96 Swiss francs. "Investors we spoke with were similarly surprised and in our view the most likely explanation is profit-taking on the back of strong share price appreciation (+20% over last three months), compounded by lack of liquidity," analysts write. They also point to the bank's acquisition of Cite Gestion which they say reflects EFG's constructive take on M&A. Compared with peers Julius Baer and Vontobel, which have done select deals for capabilities, EFG is the company most focused on acquiring for scale purposes, they add. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0153 ET - London Stock Exchange Group's shares are set up well to perform this year, RBC Capital Markets analysts Ben Bathurst and Jude Neanor write in a note. The stock-exchange and financial-information company is expected to deliver sustained strong underlying earnings and free cash-flow growth, supported by recent investments and acquisitions, the analysts say. "Given strong expected cash generation and leverage at the middle of the target range, we also can envisage a further capital return of around 1 billion pounds in buybacks," the Canadian bank adds. Shares closed at 117.05 pounds on Wednesday. ( najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

data.title: Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1520 ET - Crude futures hold onto moderate gains as near-term supply concerns are partly offset by a bigger-than-expected 4.6 million barrel increase in U.S. crude stockpiles despite a drop in net imports. "April WTI crude oil is seemingly willing to discount the big build, the EIA premium to the API, and the Line 16 adjustment number and instead concentrate on those rather impressive import/export dynamics," Mizuho's Robert Yawger say in a note.While imports fell by 488,000 barrels a day last week, exports grew by 472,000 b/d. March WTI goes off the board at $72.57 a barrel, up 0.4%, and the April contract rises 0.5% to $72.48. Brent gains 0.6% to $76.48 a barrel. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1410 ET - Innergex Renewable had a standout quarter, ending the year on a strong note thanks to the wind segment. Scotiabank's Robert Hope says in a research report that Ebitda proportionate came to C$210 million, topping consensus estimates of C$180 million. "The Wind segment was the key driver of the beat, though it appears that the majority of the beat was driven by one-time items," Hope says. For the year ahead, management expectations includes Ebitda proportionate of C$825 million-C$875 million and distributable cash flow per share of C$0.75-C$0.95. "Our EBITDA estimate of C$868 million is towards the top end of the range, while our distributable cash flow estimate of C$0.80 is in the bottom half of the range," the analyst says. Shares are up 8.6% to C$8.36.(adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

1254 ET - Mexico's reliance on U.S. piped gas will continue to grow, Fitch Ratings says, even as uncertainties over bilateral trade relations increase. U.S. gas imports provide reliable and cost-effective energy for Mexico, buoyed by rising demand, constrained domestic production, and pipeline infrastructure expansion, Fitch says. The market share of U.S. piped gas in Mexico is expected to increase in the coming years, according to Fitch. Trade uncertainties have emerged since President Trump came into office, but the administration has mainly focused on Mexico's exports to the U.S., and Fitch says it sees policy-driven disruptions to the gas trade as a smaller risk. (stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com)

0934 ET - Reduced oil supply from Kazakhstan and production shut-ins due to cold weather in North Dakota support oil futures, which are higher in early trade. "Add in OPEC+ members stating further delays are likely in its April production increase and the crude price positives near term seem to be outweighing the negatives," BOK Financial's Dennis Kissler says in a note. Verbal conflicts between President Trump and Ukrainian President Zelensky could delay a Russian peace deal, he adds. WTI is up 0.7% at $72.74 a barrel ahead of today's March expiration. Brent is up 0.7% at $76.60 a barrel. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

0547 ET - Repsol's outlook for 2025 is supportive for shares, and so too is the company's buyback plan, UBS analysts say in research note. The Spanish energy company's plan for a minimum 700 million euro buyback is 13% higher than consensus expectations of 618 million euros, the analyst say. The company is also guiding for fewer capital expenditures than were expected, which should also support Repsol's shares, they say. Operational cash flow of 6.0 billion to 6.5 billion euros, however, missed UBS forecasts, but the guidance exceeds consensus, the analysts say. Shares trade up 5.2% at 12.81 euros. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0542 ET - Centrica posted lower profit and revenue for the year, as energy markets normalized and one-off recovery of costs in its British Gas division didn't repeat, AJ Bell investment director Russ Mould says in a market comment. But the energy company's prior year was always going to be a tough act to follow, he says. "However, it's all about expectations for the market and Centrica came in ahead of what had been penciled in, which, along with retaining its 2025 forecast, was sufficient to get investors energized," he says. Shares trade 7.8% higher at 146.45 pence. (nina.kienle@wsj.com)

0531 ET - Naturgy is using its balance sheet to help fix its reference shareholders' issues, analysts at RBC Capital Markets say in a research note. The Spanish natural-gas and electricity utility said it is launching a tender offer for up to 10% of its capital with the aim of increasing its free float. It guides for net debt of 16 billion euros in 2027, up from 12.2 billion euros at the end of 2024. Naturgy was the subject of a takeover attempt last year by Abu Dhabi's Taqa that failed after the suitor didn't reach an agreement with CriteriaCaixa, Naturgy's biggest shareholder. Taqa had sought to cooperate with CriteriaCaixa and two other big Naturgy shareholders--CVC Capital Partners and Global Infrastructure Partners, which is now part of BlackRock--on a possible takeover. Shares rise 4.6%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0444 ET - Centrica posted an overall positive update, Jefferies analysts say in a research note. The U.K. energy company, which owns British Gas, said its was extending its share buyback and provided clearer information on capital allocation. Earnings per share and net cash came in above consensus estimates and the dividend payout was in line, the analysts say. Centrica also gave a detailed plan on how to reduce net debt, the analysts say. Shares trade 8% higher at 146.70 pence. (nina.kienle@wsj.com)

0402 ET - Naturgy Energy Group's cash-flow performance allows the company to raise its dividend target in its 2025-27 plan, Renta4's Angel Perez Llamazares says in a research note. The Spanish utility company's results exceed Renta4's expectations on most metrics despite lower energy prices, the analyst says. Cash flow was supported by the group's networks, renewables and commercialization operations and allowed the company to maintain debt broadly stable from a year before, Renta4 says. Naturgy said it revised its dividend policy for the coming years, targeting an increase in its payout to 1.90 euros a share in 2027 from 1.60 euros a share in 2024. Shares rise 4.1%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0318 ET - Repsol's adjusted profit came in higher than expected driven by the company's downstream segments and the Spanish energy company's plan for a minimum 700 million euro buyback this year exceeded RBC Capital Markets' expectation, analysts Biraj Borkhataria and Adnan Dhanani say in a research note. The company forecasts production this year to fall more than expected by consensus due to divestments and less unconventional hydrocarbon production in the U.S., the analysts say. "We think expectations were low heading into [the results] as refining margins over recent months have been relatively soft versus prior years," they say. Shares are up 3.7% at 12.62 euros. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0253 ET - PTT Exploration and Production's sales volume growth may help offset oil-price headwinds, Maybank Securities (Thailand)'s Chak Reungsinpinya says in a research report as the brokerage maintains the stock's buy rating. The Thai company's 2025-2026 sales volume growth seems highly secured as most of this stems from projects in Thailand and recently acquired assets, the analyst says. However, OPEC+ will probably maintain its oil-production increase plan, at least in the initial stage, which could exacerbate potential oversupply and result in a lower oil-price environment. The brokerage lowers its 2025-2028 earnings estimates for the oil-and-gas company by 6%-11% to partly reflect higher unit-cost assumptions. It cuts the stock's target price to THB141.00 from THB158.00. Shares are 1.2% lower at THB126.50. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2251 ET - Citicore Renewable Energy looks well-placed to drive the Philippines' energy transition to renewable sources via its pure-play domestic strategy, Maybank Securities' Germaine Guinto says in a research report. The government is targeting 35% renewable-energy share of power generation by 2030, with Citicore Renewable Energy expected to contribute at least 15%, the analyst notes. The Philippine renewable energy company currently has 285 megawatts of gross installed capacity, says the brokerage, which expects it to achieve 4 gigawatts of operational capacity by 2028. Maybank Securities initiates coverage of the stock with a buy rating and a target price of PHP4.50. Shares are 2.0% lower at PHP3.42. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Codan 1H Revenue A$305.6 Million, Up 15% on Year
Codan 1H Net Profit A$46.0 Million, Up 21% on Year
Boise Cascade Profit Falls as Homebuilding Slows
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Boise Cascade profit dipped 29% in the fourth quarter as fewer homes began construction in the U.S.

The maker of wood products on Thursday posted a profit of $68.9 million, or $1.78 a share, in the three months ended Dec. 31, compared with $97.5 million, or $2.44 a share, a year earlier.

The profit was slightly below the $70 million, or $1.79 a share, that analysts expected, according to FactSet.

Revenue fell 5% to $1.57 billion, in line with what analysts polled by FactSet expected.

Boise Cascade logged a 7% decline in wood products sales and a 4% fall in building materials distribution sales.

U.S. housing starts decreased in the fourth quarter, which is a key demand driver for the company's sales, it said. Over the full year, housing starts declined 4%, with single-family housing starts down 7%.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Basic Materials Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Basic Materials. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1401 ET - Ferrexpo says it has "not received any formal notification" that the Ukrainian government has initiated proceedings to nationalize its Poltava mining and processing plant. The London-based miner adds it is working with its legal advisors to understand the situation. The statement comes after media reports that the Ukrainian State Bureau of Investigations is seeking court approval to nationalize the mine and its assets, which are a subsidiary of Ferrexpo, because of the alleged misappropriation of around $8.86 million related to illegal mining. Shares fall 30%, to 69 pence. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1006 ET - Shares in London-listed miner Ferrexpo plunged in mid-afternoon trade on reports that the Ukrainian government has initiated proceedings to nationalize its Poltava mining and processing plant. The mine and its assets are a subsidiary of Ferrexpo. The Ukrainian State Bureau of Investigations is seeking court approval to nationalize the assets, citing the alleged misappropriation of around $8.86 million related to illegal mining, Reuters reported earlier today. Shares fell 28% to 72.10 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0532 ET - Anglo American's hopes of finding a buyer for its De Beers diamond unit in the near term are slim, AJ Bell investment director Russ Mould writes. This is because the outlook for De Beers is so bleak as lab-grown diamonds have flooded the market, he writes. Despite the $2.9 billion write down, investors have focused on the rapid progress the London-listed miner has made since rebuffing a bid from BHP last year, Mould writes. Investors like the streamlined business but it could make it more vulnerable to further bid interest, he adds. Shares trade up 3% at 2,442.00 pence and are up 38% over the past year.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0518 ET - Rio Tinto's earnings generation proves its diversified model works, Jefferies analysts write. The London and Sydney listed miner reported a 2% fall in earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization despite an 11% decrease in benchmark iron ore prices, they write. While other miners turn their attention towards copper, Rio is positioned to continue generating strong cash flow from iron ore despite its weak price, they write. Cash flow from base iron ore will enable Rio to continue to pay a healthy dividend even in a weaker macro environment, and should make the miner relatively defensive versus its peers, they write. The analysts like Rio's defensive position and believe its shares are undervalued. Rio Tinto's London listed shares rose 0.75% to 5,074.00 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0441 ET - Rio Tinto's 2024 results were broadly in line with expectations and the Anglo-Australian mining company provided a steady outlook for the future, energy and materials analyst at Quilter Cheviot Maurizio Carulli writes. It is reassuring to see that group earnings decreased by only 2% despite an 8% fall in iron-ore prices, he writes. While acquisitions pushed net debt up to $5.5 billion in 2024, it remains at comfortable level, he says. The miner boasts a solid portfolio of assets and the significant barriers to entry in iron ore and copper strengthen Rio Tinto's market position, he writes. Rio Tinto's London listed shares rise 0.75% to 5,074.00 pence.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0359 ET - Anglo American's $2.9 billion write down of its De Beers unit was in-line with expectations, RBC Capital Markets Ben Davis and Marina Calero write. Anglo's simplification efforts mean it is no longer a bid target given its share-price rise, the analysts write. The shares trade up 38% over the past year and only trade at an around 2.5% discount to the final offer BHP made last year, they add. The miner also made a successful step forward by agreeing a memorandum of understanding with Codelco to implement a joint mine plan between Los Bronces and Andina, which will deliver straightforward synergies, they write. Shares trade up 3.65% at 2,456.50 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for May delivery falls 28 ringgit to 4,644 ringgit a ton. "Palm Oil Falls, Tracking Soybean Oil Weakness — Market Talk," at 0238 GMT, incorrectly said it fell 63 ringgit to 4,567 ringgit a ton.

2138 ET - Palm oil prices fall in Asian trading, mirroring weakness in soybean oil overnight on the Chicago Board of Trade. However, palm oil prices are expected to stay firm in the near term, supported by limited supply, Abdul Hameed, director of sales at Pakistan-based Manzoor Trading says. He notes that rising demand ahead of festivals, including Ramadan, especially in the Central Asia region, and shrinking global inventories, may support prices. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for May delivery falls 28 ringgit to 4,644 ringgit a ton. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com) Corrections & Amplifications

This market talk item was corrected at 0249 GMT to reflect the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for May delivery falls 28 ringgit to 4,644 ringgit a ton. The original version incorrectly said it fell 63 ringgit to 4,567 ringgit a ton

1806 ET - The market will appreciate some moderation in Fortescue's spending on energy projects, Citi analyst Paul McTaggart says in a note. The iron-ore miner earlier pared FY25 energy capex guidance to US$400 million from US$500 million. "That said, market will want to see energy capex and opex cut further," McTaggart says. Citi has a neutral rating and A$21.00 target on Fortescue. McTaggart reckons the miner's 1H fiscal result will be neutral to mildly negative for its shares. The stock last traded at A$19.45/share. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

1803 ET - Rio Tinto's cost guidance disappoints RBC Capital Markets analyst Kaan Peker, who was expecting a bigger improvement in costs given the ramp up in volumes at the Oyu Tolgoi and Kennecott mines. In a note, Peker also raises concerns about first ore from Simandou, expected later this year, and the possible impact that will have on the iron-ore market. Otherwise, Rio Tinto is delivering on its strategy and has one of the better growth rates among major miners, with a focus on copper, iron ore and lithium, Peker says. RBC has a sector perform rating and A$120 target on Rio Tinto, which last traded at A$121.95/share. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

1741 ET - Fortescue's 1H Ebitda misses consensus by roughly 3% because of higher-than-anticipated operating costs, Jefferies analyst Mitch Ryan says in note. Although, an interim dividend of A$0.50/share is a beat versus consensus of A$0.35/share, he says. "Iron-ore demand remains the key near-term catalyst, with emphasis on China policy decisions from the Two-Sessions mid-March, 1Q 2025 GDP release mid-April, and Politburo meeting in April," Ryan says. Jefferies has a hold rating and A$18.50 target on Fortescue. The stock last traded at A$19.45/share. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

data.title: Auto & Transport Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Auto and Transport sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0830 ET - Mercedes-Benz's passenger-cars guidance for 2025 is broadly in line with expectations, Bernstein analysts write in a research note. The German luxury-car maker anticipates an adjusted return on sales for its passenger cars division from 6% to 8%. This matches consensus estimates of 7.1%, Bernstein says. For its vans business, the group forecasts an adjusted return on sales between 10% and 12%, below consensus of 13.3%, they say. The group's passenger cars outlook was predictably weak, but the vans guidance was softer than anticipated, the analysts say. Shares are down 1.7% at 60.13 euros. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

0605 ET - Mercedes-Benz Group's fourth-quarter results and outlook are better than feared by some investors, Morgan Stanley's Javier Martinez de Olcoz Cerdan and Shaqeal A Kirunda say in a research note. The German luxury-car maker's quarterly earnings topped expectations, driven by a higher proportion of top-end sales that helped profitability, the analysts say. Its guidance for the year ahead is relatively in line with consensus estimates, Morgan Stanley says. However, investors' focus is on the company's plans to restore profitability, the bank says. "The sector faces cyclical, structural, and geopolitical headwinds that could last several years. [Mercedes] is not immune to those, and consensus has just partially reflected these, we think," the analysts say. Shares fall 1.9%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0520 ET - Renault's performance for 2024 is reassuring given how most other European original equipment makers issued warnings last year, UBS analysts David Lesne and Patrick Hummel say in a research note. The French carmaker's second-half EBIT for its automotive division came in line with consensus expectations due to stronger volume growth and lower negative currency effects, the analysts say. Despite the result, UBS says it doesn't expect consensus expectations for 2025 to change much due to the lack of visibility on the effect of Europe's emissions regulation. Should the rules be delayed, or removed, consensus could rise by around 10%, UBS says. Renault shares trade 0.08% higher at 51.24 euros. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0423 ET - Mercedes-Benz reported solid free cash flow, but group full-year guidance is below expectations, Stifel analyst Daniel Schwarz writes. "Against...the lack of positive surprises in the results as well as a weaker outlook, we would expect a slightly negative reaction initially." However, more important will be news out of the capital markets day later today, he says. Mercedes is guiding for group revenue slightly below prior-year level, group EBIT and free cash flow significantly below prior-year level. For EBIT, "significantly" means a more than 15% decline, which implies EBIT below 11.6 billion euros. Consensus assumes 12.2 billion euros group EBIT in 2025, Schwarz says. Shares fall 2.6% to 59.59 euros. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0406 ET - Mercedes-Benz fourth quarter is better than expected all around, Jefferies analysts write. Despite downbeat guidance, there are no major surprises. The company sees 2025 EBIT and free cash flow "significantly" below 2024 while guidance for the vans unit is below expectations. The 4.30 euro dividend and 5 billion euro planned buyback keeps free cash flow distribution at 100%. There are no details yet on mid-term guidance but the company will present a strategic update later today. Shares fall 3.2% to 59.22 euros. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0254 ET - Mercedes-Benz shares could come under some selling pressure today, RBC Capital Markets analyst Tom Narayan writes. Some may have expected the company's earnings to offer more on capital return as it relates to the Daimler Truck stake. Additionally, the van margin guidance for 2025 was below consensus. However, the fourth quarter cars EBIT came in ahead of consensus. More importantly, the company is calling for 2025 cars EBIT margins to be 7% at the midpoint, in line with consensus. Fourth-quarter U.S. volumes were up 8.5% and impressively. APAC was up slightly on year. Margin beat guidance thanks to improving China performance, model availability and better S-Class sales. Shares closed at 61.18 euros. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0211 ET - Malaysia's central bank may cut its policy rate by 25 bps in May, Barclays economist Brian Tan says, though the timing remains uncertain and dependant on U.S. tariff announcements. BNM reacted quickly to cut during the 2019 Trump tariff era and could do so again if it assesses that U.S. tariffs will hit Malaysia hard, he notes. Malaysia's 4Q GDP print showed a sequential contraction and the backdrop is likely to darken once more tariffs materialize, he says. Consensus sees concerns about the inflationary impact of fuel subsidy reforms as a constraint to rate cuts, but Barclays argues that BNM prioritizes broader economic conditions over inflation targeting. As growth cools and risks rise, it is unlikely to be so confident on the Malaysian economy that it will look past tariffs, Tan says. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2153 ET - Airports of Thailand's share-price slump this year probably reflects bad news about delayed payments from its key duty-free concessionaire King Power, Thanachart Securities' Saksid Phadthananarak says in a research report. AOT's passenger recovery is poised to stay strong, as the number of its international passengers grew 17% on year during the first four months of FY 2025, the analyst says. Moreover, there's upside earnings risk from possible passenger service charge increases, says the brokerage which maintains the stock's buy rating. However, given King Power's delayed payments, the brokerage still needs to factor in some counterparty risk and lowers the target price to THB55.00 from THB70.00. Shares closed 0.6% lower at THB43.25 on Wednesday. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2142 ET - Bintulu Port may see strong 4Q earnings, driven by deferred cargo deliveries and winter demand, AmInvestment Bank analyst Muhammad Nuur Ashman Ab Razak says in a note. Liquefied natural gas exports rebounded, supported by heat waves in Japan and South Korea, while non-LNG exports saw recovery in alumina, manganese, and quartz, he says. The analyst expects steady LNG throughput moving forward, supported by new gas fields, but flags uncertainty over LNG contract renewals beyond 2025. Non-LNG growth will be driven by new production from industrial plants in Bintulu, boosting port utilization, he adds. AmInvestment Bank maintains a hold rating on Bintulu Port and keeps the target price at MYR6.40. Shares are 1.3% lower at MYR6.00. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Annexon CFO Jennifer Lew Sells Shares to Cover Tax Obligations
data.description:
Reporter NameLew Jennifer
RelationshipEVP & CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER
TypeSell
Amount$20,183
SEC FilingForm 4

Jennifer Lew, EVP & Chief Financial Officer of Annexon, sold 6,912 shares of common stock on February 18, 2025, at a weighted average price of $2.92 per share, totaling $20,183. The sale was conducted to cover tax withholding obligations related to the vesting of restricted stock units. Following the transaction, Lew directly owns 77,770 shares of Annexon's common stock.

SEC Filing: Annexon, Inc. [ ANNX ] - Form 4 - Feb. 20, 2025

data.title: AIC Mines Price Target Cut 9.1% to A$1.00/Share by Shaw & Partners
Floor & Decor Q4 Earnings, Revenue Rise; 2025 Outlook Initiated -- Shares Jump After Hours
data.description:
Floor & Decor reported Q4 diluted earnings Thursday of $0.44 per share, up from $0.34 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected $0.26.

Revenue for the quarter ended Dec. 26 was $1.11 billion, up from $1.05 billion a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $1.09 billion.

The company expects full-year 2025 diluted earnings of $1.80 to $2.10 per share on revenue of $4.74 billion to $4.90 billion.

Analysts polled by FactSet expect $1.91 on revenue of $4.85 billion.

Shares jumped 12% in recent after-hours activity.

data.title: ROSEN, SKILLED INVESTOR COUNSEL, Encourages Merck & Co., Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action – MRK
data.description:
NEW YORK, Feb. 20, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) —

WHY: Rosen Law Firm, a global investor rights law firm, reminds purchasers of securities of Merck & Co., Inc. between February 3, 2022 and February 3, 2025, both dates inclusive (the “Class Period”), of the important April 14, 2025 lead plaintiff deadline.

SO WHAT: If you purchased Merck securities during the Class Period you may be entitled to compensation without payment of any out of pocket fees or costs through a contingency fee arrangement.

WHAT TO DO NEXT: To join the Merck class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=34975 or call Phillip Kim, Esq. toll-free at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for information on the class action. A class action lawsuit has already been filed. If you wish to serve as lead plaintiff, you must move the Court no later than April 14, 2025. A lead plaintiff is a representative party acting on behalf of other class members in directing the litigation.

WHY ROSEN LAW: We encourage investors to select qualified counsel with a track record of success in leadership roles. Often, firms issuing notices do not have comparable experience, resources, or any meaningful peer recognition. Many of these firms do not actually litigate securities class actions, but are merely middlemen that refer clients or partner with law firms that actually litigate the cases. Be wise in selecting counsel. The Rosen Law Firm represents investors throughout the globe, concentrating its practice in securities class actions and shareholder derivative litigation. Rosen Law Firm achieved the largest ever securities class action settlement against a Chinese Company at the time. Rosen Law Firm was Ranked No. 1 by ISS Securities Class Action Services for number of securities class action settlements in 2017. The firm has been ranked in the top 4 each year since 2013 and has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for investors. In 2019 alone the firm secured over $438 million for investors. In 2020, founding partner Laurence Rosen was named by law360 as a Titan of Plaintiffs’ Bar. Many of the firm’s attorneys have been recognized by Lawdragon and Super Lawyers.

DETAILS OF THE CASE: According to the lawsuit, throughout the Class Period, defendants provided investors with material information concerning Merck’s expected revenue of $11 billion from sales of Gardasil by 2030. Defendants’ statements included, among other things, confidence in Merck’s purported ability to utilize successful consumer activation and education efforts on the benefits of Gardasil in order to drive demand and capitalize on eligible populations for vaccination, resulting in confidently optimistic reports and forecasts of Gardasil’s growth in China. Defendants provided these overwhelmingly positive statements to investors while, at the same time, disseminating materially false and misleading statements and/or concealing material adverse facts concerning the true state of Gardasil’s demand in China; notably, that Merck lacked visibility into demand for Gardasil in China among eligible and otherwise targeted populations, resulting in the inflated inventory of its distributor, Zhifei. When the true details entered the market, the lawsuit claims that investors suffered damages.

To join the Merck class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=34975 call Phillip Kim, Esq. toll-free at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for information on the class action.

No Class Has Been Certified. Until a class is certified, you are not represented by counsel unless you retain one. You may select counsel of your choice. You may also remain an absent class member and do nothing at this point. An investor’s ability to share in any potential future recovery is not dependent upon serving as lead plaintiff.

Follow us for updates on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/the-rosen-law-firm or on Twitter: https://twitter.com/rosen_firm or on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/rosenlawfirm.

Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee a similar outcome.

Contact Information:

        Laurence Rosen, Esq.

        Phillip Kim, Esq.

        The Rosen Law Firm, P.A.

        275 Madison Avenue, 40th Floor

        New York, NY 10016

        Tel: (212) 686-1060

        Toll Free: (866) 767-3653

        Fax: (212) 202-3827

        case@rosenlegal.com

        www.rosenlegal.com

data.title: Consolidated Edison Files 8K - Operations And Financial Condition >ED
data.description:
Consolidated Edison Inc. (ED) filed a Form 8K - Operations and Financial Condition - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on February 20, 2025.

On February 20, 2025, Consolidated Edison, Inc. is issuing a press release and an earnings release presentation regarding, among other things, its results of operations for the three months and year ended December 31, 2024. The press release and the earnings release presentation are "furnished" as exhibits to this report pursuant to Item 2.02 of Form 8-K.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1047862/000104786225000014/ed-20250220.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1047862/000104786225000014/0001047862-25-000014-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: Codan Interim Dividend 12.5 Australian Cents/Security
Newmont Up 0.7% In US After Hours Trading On a Q4 Results Beat and Bullish Outlook
data.description:
Newmont , the No.1 gold miner was at last look up 0.7% in after-hours trading as it reported adjusted earnings for the fourth quarter more than tripled from a year earlier and delivered a bullish outlook.

The company reported an adjusted profit, excluding most one-time items, of near US$1.6 billion, or US$1.40 per share, up from US$452 million, or US$0.46, a year earlier. It easily beat the consensus forecast of US$0.91 according to FactSet.

Revenue rose 43% to US$5.65 billion from US$3.96 billion.

Among highlights, Newmont in 2024 produced 6.8-million attributable gold ounces, primarily driven by production of 5.7-million attributable gold ounces (GEOs) from Newmont's Tier 1 Portfolio, as well as 1.9-million GEOs from copper, silver, lead and zinc, including 153-thousand tonnes of copper.

Separately, Newmont reported gold Mineral Reserves of 134.1-million attributable ounces at the end of 2024 compared to the company's 135.9-million attributable ounces a year earlier, inclusive of assets held for divestment. Newmont added its go-forward Tier 1 portfolio includes 125.5-million attributable gold ounces and significant Mineral Reserves from other metals, including more than 13.-million attributable tonnes of copper reserves and 530 million attributable ounces of silver reserves.

On 2025 guidance, attributable production for 2025 is expected to be approximately 5.9-million gold ounces, including 0.3-million gold ounces in the first quarter from the non-core assets held for sale and 5.6-million gold ounces for the Total Tier 1 Portfolio.

Also, gold all-in sustaining costs (AISC) for the Total Portfolio is expected to be US$1,630 per ounce including production from non-core assets for the first quarter of 2025; with Gold AISC from the Total Tier 1 Portfolio expected to be US$1,620 per ounce for 2025.

2025 guidance also targets sustaining capital spending of approximately US$1.8 billion in 2025 for the Total Tier 1 Portfolio; Development capital spending of approximately US$1.3 billion in 2025 for the Total Tier 1 Portfolio to progress key near-term development projects.

"2024 was a transformational year for Newmont, as we focused on the integration of the Newcrest portfolio, divestment of our non-core assets, and transitioning the business onto a stable operating and investment platform. We have deliberately streamlined Newmont into the world's best collection of Tier 1 gold assets, with a strong foundation of operational and financial performance. Our record fourth quarter gave a glimpse into the promising potential of the business and allowed Newmont to deliver record operating cash flows," said chief executive Tom Palmer.

"With the gold price predicted to remain strong and the proceeds from our divestiture program expected to materialize during the first half of 2025, we expect our balance sheet and liquidity remains robust. This year we are focused on continuing to improve the business across our safety, costs, and productivity performance. Looking to 2025 and beyond, our priorities are clear: maximize the potential of our Tier 1 portfolio, meet our commitments, return capital, and drive long-term value for our shareholders," Palmer added.

The company's shares were last seen up US$0.32 to US$48.41 after hours. They closed up $0.68, or 1%, to $68.14 on the Toronto Stock Exchange.

data.title: US real estate firm CoStar offers to buy Australia's Domain in $1.7 billion deal
data.description:
Australia's Domain Holdings said on Friday that U.S. online real estate firm CoStar had offered to buy the property classifieds firm in a deal valuing it at A$2.65 billion ($1.70 billion).

($1 = 1.5623 Australian dollars)

data.title: Nu Holdings Posts Hike in Q4 Net Income, Total Revenue
data.description:
Nu Holdings late Thursday reported Q4 adjusted net income of $610.1 million compared with $395.8 million a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet, on average, expected $588.4 million.

Revenue, calculated as the sum of net interest income and non-interest income, was $2.99 billion during the three months ended Dec. 31, compared with $2.40 billion for the bank company a year ago.

Analysts expected $2.76 billion.
